data.body:
Knots should add a stable native step execution record and expose it consistently via show --json.

Native Knots schema
Define a canonical step record shape, for example:

id
step
phase
from_state
to_state
status (started, completed, aborted, failed)
actor_kind
agent_id
agent_name
agent_model
agent_version
agent_command
session_id
started_at
ended_at
metadata for extensibility
Native Knots behavior
Knots should write one of these records whenever a step is claimed or advanced, not just freeform notes:

On claim, create or update a step record for the step being entered, with agent identity from the CLI flags.
On next, finalize the active step record with the resulting state transition and end timestamp.
If the same step is retried, create a new record rather than mutating history away.
If the active step changes ownership or agent mid-flight, record that as a new step execution entry, not just an overwrite.
Knots CLI/API surface
Add explicit native support rather than relying on implicit side effects:

kno show <id> --json should always expose a single canonical field, preferably step_history.
kno claim and kno poll --claim should continue accepting --agent-name, --agent-model, --agent-version, and should persist those onto the new step record.
kno next should attach completion data to the active step record.
If needed, add an explicit command such as kno step record or kno step annotate for cases where the executing agent differs inside one orchestration session and Knots needs an explicit override from Foolery.
Knots acceptance criteria
Before Foolery work starts, Knots should satisfy:

A fresh claim creates a structured step record with agent/model/version.
Transitioning out of the step closes that record with to_state and ended_at.
Repeated action/review cycles produce multiple ordered records.
show --json returns the same canonical field every time.
The data is sufficient to answer “who executed implementation_review for this knot, and with which model?”
data.title: Step History Model
